title: Lilypad
description: 1.21.8 - 1.21.10 | Minimalist by design. Built for server flawless performance.
body:
![lilypadsbanner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bcf0eaabd5d7a8ee39bfa66351edf88c8a34df2b.png)
## **Why Lilypad?**

Lilypad is built for servers that need **speed and stability**. No clutter, no unnecessary mods — just what’s essential for a smooth, reliable Minecraft experience. Its carefully chosen mods ensure that **vanilla players can still join** with no extra downloads required. Lilypad also includes **bug fixes** to make gameplay smoother and more consistent.

(**Lilypad is best used on servers. If you want to boost your client's performance, check out [Ultralight](https://modrinth.com/modpack/ultralight)!**)

## **Lilypad is Minimalist**

Every addition is intentional. Our modpack is **lean, clean, and optimized**, so your server runs faster without sacrificing gameplay. 

## **What’s the Difference?**

Unlike most optimization modpacks, **Lilypad is built specifically for servers**. Many packs include mods that don’t even impact server performance — Lilypad doesn’t. Every mod included is **chosen to enhance server efficiency and stability**, nothing more.

## **Lilypad Enhances, Not Changes**

We stay **faithful to Minecraft’s core mechanics**. You’ll notice improvements and polish, but the game you love remains intact. 

## **Lilypad is Server-Ready**

From small worlds to large communities, Lilypad is **designed to perform** under heavy load, keeping your server responsive and lag-free. 

## **Lilypad Makes Setup Simple**

No complicated tweaks or heavy configs. **Install and play** — everything works together seamlessly from the start. 

## Updating the Modpack & Tags

Before we upload a new Lilypad update, we assess performance under normal conditions. If the update meets our internal performance targets, it will be released with an **“A”** in the version name.
If it fails to meet those targets, it will be released with an **“F”** in the version name.

**Tags**

* **A** — Stable and user-ready; no major issues.
* **B** — Acceptable but below optimal performance targets (FPS and/or frametime slightly off).
* **F** — Performance issues; FPS and/or frametime significantly below target.
